synopsis: Western Carolina Presbytery and two other presbyteries requested the General Assembly to assume original jurisdiction over a teaching elder regarding allegations that women preachers filled the pulpit at Cedar Springs Presbyterian Church. The Standing Judicial Commission dismissed the matter as not properly before the General Assembly, ruling that the conduct of worship services is under the jurisdiction of the Session, not subject to process against a minister under BCO 34-1, and that Tennessee Valley Presbytery did act on the allegations.
